region_of_origin: Poland (served in Singapore as fusion dish)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of fried pierogi is 6 pieces, weighing approximately 180 g, typically served as a snack or appetizer.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pierogies, potato and cheese filled, frozen (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entries for bacon, cheddar cheese, and deep-frying fat absorption data
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for deep-fried pierogi with smoked bacon, truffle potato, caramelized onion, cheddar cheese, and sriracha crema. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for potato-and-cheese pierogi (FDC ID 172004) combined with deep-frying fat absorption adjustments and component-level data for smoked bacon and cheddar cheese. Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA composite estimate and retained where consistent. Energy 322 kcal/100g, protein 12.2g, fat 22.2g, carbohydrate 16.7g are plausible for a deep-fried filled dumpling with cheese and bacon filling.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous food: boiled potato-filled dumplings / pasta with potato filling (GI ~65–70)
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for deep-fried pierogi. GI estimated at 66 based on analogous foods: boiled potato-filled pasta/dumplings have a reported GI of approximately 65–70 (glucose reference) in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Deep-frying increases fat content which may modestly lower GI relative to boiled versions; however, the wheat-flour dough and potato filling remain the dominant glycemic drivers. GI of 66 (medium) is a conservative estimate. GL per serving = (66 × 16.67 × 1.80) / 100 = 19.9 g per 180 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 527.8 mg/100g from smoked bacon, cheddar cheese, and sriracha crema; saturated fat 8.33 g/100g contributes to arterial stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit fried pierogi due to high sodium content (527.8 mg/100g); a single 180 g serving delivers approximately 950 mg sodium, representing over 40% of the 2300 mg daily limit, and the saturated fat load further elevates cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (66) with 16.7 g carbohydrate/100g from refined wheat dough and potato filling; high saturated fat (8.33 g/100g) worsens insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit fried pierogi due to a medium glycemic index of 66 and a glycemic load of approximately 19.9 per serving, combined with high saturated fat that impairs insulin sensitivity; if consumed, portion should be restricted to 2–3 pieces and paired with non-starchy vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.33 g/100g, trans fat 0.28 g/100g, cholesterol 41.67 mg/100g, and high sodium from smoked bacon and cheddar cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid fried pierogi due to the combination of high saturated fat (8.33 g/100g), trans fat (0.28 g/100g), and elevated sodium (527.8 mg/100g), all of which contribute to LDL elevation, endothelial dysfunction, and increased blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); smoked bacon is a pro-inflammatory processed red meat; saturated fat 8.33 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid fried pierogi as deep-frying generates AGEs that promote joint inflammation, and the smoked bacon and high saturated fat content further drive systemic pro-inflammatory pathways.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fried Pierogi are deep-fried Eastern European dumplings adapted as a fusion snack dish. The wheat-flour dough casing is filled with a rich mixture of smoked bacon, truffle-infused mashed potato, caramelized onion, and cheddar cheese, then deep-fried until golden and crisp. The exterior is crunchy with a slightly chewy inner dough layer, while the filling is savory, creamy, and umami-rich from the truffle and bacon. The dish is served with a spicy sriracha crema dipping sauce. Nutritionally, each 100 g delivers approximately 322 kcal, 12.2 g protein, 22.2 g fat (8.3 g saturated), and 16.7 g carbohydrate. Sodium is high at 527.8 mg/100g due to smoked bacon, cheddar, and sriracha. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 19.9 per 180 g serving. This dish is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and moderate glycemic load. The deep-frying process also generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), making it a concern for rheumatoid arthritis patients. Calcium is present at 83.3 mg/100g from cheddar cheese. The dish is not halal due to smoked bacon and contains gluten, dairy, and possible egg allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry exists for deep-fried pierogi with this specific filling combination. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central potato-and-cheese pierogi data combined with component-level adjustments for smoked bacon, cheddar, and deep-frying fat absorption. Input record values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked and found plausible. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific deep-fried pierogi formulation (smoked bacon, truffle potato, cheddar, caramelized onion, sriracha crema) are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. The input record cultural metadata incorrectly classified this dish as Chinese cuisine with ethnic tags including Noodle and Ramen; these have been corrected — pierogi is an Eastern European (Polish-origin) dish served here as a fusion snack. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was listed under HPB Singapore but does not constitute an approved nutrient source for this dish; USDA FoodData Central was used instead. No zero-value core macros are present in this record. Trans fat is reported as 0.28 g/100g (non-zero, from smoked bacon and frying oil). All core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a deep-fried filled dumpling.
